notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for france. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

chunks.title: France tourism/private stay scope
chunks.content: France-Visas separates tourism and private stay from professional, study/training, and family-purpose routes. Stays for private reasons or tourism do not allow the traveller to exercise an activity related to a professional reason, except for specific working holiday eligibility. For VIZA AI: if the user wants tourism in France for under 3 months, use the France short-stay tourism/private stay route; if they mention work, study, family migration, volunteering, or medical care, ask follow-up questions and avoid forcing the tourism route.

chunks.title: France visa wizard and online application
chunks.content: France-Visas tells applicants to assess their situation with the France-Visas online support/wizard to identify the required supporting documents, then start the visa process online after checking whether a visa is needed. Supporting documents depend on nationality, residence country, destination territory, and purpose.

chunks.title: 中国护照法国短期旅游签证建议
chunks.content: 中国护照持有人如果去法国短期旅游、探亲访友或私人访问，且法国是唯一目的地或主要停留国家，通常应按法国短期 Schengen 旅游/私人访问路线准备，而不是越南 e-visa、英国 Standard Visitor 或美国 DS-160。VIZA AI 应先确认国籍、居住地、法国是否为主目的地、停留天数、是否同时去其他申根国家；常见材料方向包括护照、申请表、照片、旅行医疗保险、行程/住宿、资金证明、回国约束力和指纹采集。

chunks.title: Where and when to apply for France/Schengen
chunks.content: A Schengen visa is for a short temporary visit of up to 90 days in any 180-day period. The EU guidance says to apply at the consulate of the country intended to visit; when visiting multiple Schengen countries, apply to the country where the traveller will spend the longest time, or the first-entry country if stays are equal. Applications should be submitted at least 15 days before travel and no earlier than 6 months before the intended journey.

chunks.title: France/Schengen documents, fees, and processing
chunks.content: Common Schengen application documents include a passport valid at least 3 months after planned departure from the Schengen area, a visa application form, ICAO-compliant photo, travel medical insurance covering emergency medical care/hospitalisation/repatriation, purpose-of-stay documents, evidence of financial means and accommodation, evidence of intention to return, and fingerprints. EU guidance lists the adult visa fee as EUR 90 and normal processing as 15 days, extendable up to 45 days if more examination or documents are needed.

documents.title: France Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: France application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Use the competent France consulate or its authorised visa application centre when France is the only destination, main destination, longest-stay destination, or first-entry state when stays are equal. Also check the country-specific source: https://www.france-visas.gouv.fr/en/tourisme-et-sejour-prive. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf ; https://www.france-visas.gouv.fr/en/tourisme-et-sejour-prive ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: France fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: The harmonised Schengen form intake should collect surname/current and birth names, first names, date/place/country of birth, current and birth nationality, sex, civil status, parental authority for minors, national ID where applicable, travel document type/number/issue/expiry/issuing authority, home address/email/phone, residence in another country if any, occupation, employer or school, travel purpose, destination Member State(s), first-entry Member State, number of entries requested, intended arrival/departure dates and duration, prior Schengen fingerprints, entry permit for final destination if any, inviting person/company or hotel/accommodation, who pays travel/living costs, EU/EEA/Swiss family member data if applicable, and applicant declaration/signature.

chunks.title: France supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: Core Schengen evidence includes signed visa application form, passport/travel document valid beyond the planned Schengen exit, photo, travel medical insurance, proof of purpose, itinerary/transport, accommodation or host invitation, evidence of financial means, evidence of intention to return, residence/jurisdiction proof, fee, and biometrics where required. Workflow guardrails: VIZA should determine the competent Schengen state before filling forms. For multi-country trips, compute longest stay or first entry if equal; collect day split and accommodation per country. Do not submit or sign declarations without explicit user confirmation.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.

chunks.title: Official-source field answer norms
chunks.content:
Official-source field answer norms for france.
Use these norms only as filling guidance; if a live official form, passport, or uploaded document shows a more specific value, that document controls the answer.

Crawled source URLs: https://www.france-visas.gouv.fr/en/tourisme-et-sejour-prive ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf

Passport and identity fields:
- The passport’s expiry date should be at least 3 months after the date of your departure from the Schengen area.

Issue, expiry, and validity fields:
- The passport’s expiry date should be at least 3 months after the date of your departure from the Schengen area.
- For multiple-entry visas, the expiry date should be at least 3 months after your departure from the last country visited.

Travel plan and port fields:
- A Schengen visa is an entry permit for non-EU nationals to make a short, temporary visit of up to 90 days in any 180-day period to a country in the Schengen area.
- single-entry visa - allowing you to enter the Schengen area once;
- multiple-entry visa - allowing several visits to the Schengen area for as long as the visa is valid;

Accommodation, host, and address fields:
- Supporting documents relating to the purpose of your stay, evidence of financial means and accommodation during your stay, and evidence of your intention to return to your home country after your stay.

Purpose, occupation, work, and study fields:
- Schengen Schengen Schengen guarantees free movement to more than 450 million EU citizens, along with non-EU nationals living in the EU or visiting the EU as tourists, exchange students or for business purposes, anyone legally present in the EU.
- Supporting documents relating to the purpose of your stay, evidence of financial means and accommodation during your stay, and evidence of your intention to return to your home country after your stay.

Funds, insurance, photo, and supporting-document fields:
- Funding Funding Learn about EU funds supporting projects on migration and home affairs: Asylum, Migration and Integration Fund (AMIF), Internal Security Fund (ISF) and Horizon Europe funding for security research.
- Asylum, migration and integration funds Asylum, Migration and Integration Fund (2021-2027)
- Internal security funds Internal Security Fund (2021-2027)

Answering rule: for closed-list fields, prefer the exact official option label/value. For document-backed fields, copy the passport, official identity document, application account, itinerary, invitation, accommodation, insurance, funds, or uploaded support document rather than guessing from a city, country, or prior answer.
